extra.reasoning: The property requires rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b never to be jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires all 12 conditions true, including asthma. rec_drug_b is set true by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever asthma is true. Since the conjunction requires asthma, any input that fires rec_a_from_conjunction necessarily also fires rec_b_from_trigger. These rules write to different outputs, so priorities (which only resolve same-output conflicts) do not prevent both from being set, and defaults do not apply because both outputs are explicitly set true. Setting all 12 conjunction conditions true (asthma, hypertension, low_ejection_fraction, qt_prolongation, diabetes, pregnancy, peptic_ulcer, hepatic_impairment, bradycardia, renal_impairment, elderly, prior_gi_bleed) yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The remaining inputs (polypharmacy, seizure_history, heart_failure) only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; set false.
